jokes.text: I once survived an entire week trapped in a Swiss glacier, eating nothing but frozen Neanderthal. To this day I can't stand the taste of early hominid.
jokes.setup: Zapp claims to know what real cold is from survival experience
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of eating ancient humans with foodie-level pickiness about taste
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zapp character comedy - combines his pompous storytelling with absurd survivalist claims and unexpected food critic sensibility

jokes.text: Crispy doesn't even rhyme with risky.
jokes.setup: Zapp recites traditional ice safety rhyme
jokes.punchline: Fry focuses on the incorrect pronunciation instead of the life-saving advice
jokes.explanation: Classic Fry behavior - missing the important point for pedantic detail. Good character work but predictable setup

jokes.text: Don't worry, son, you'll freeze before you drown.
jokes.setup: Fry is drowning and crying for help
jokes.punchline: Zapp offers 'comfort' by explaining a different way he'll die
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy delivery - perfectly captures Zapp's misguided attempt at reassurance while being hilariously unhelpful

jokes.text: I have no protective fat, hair or warm blood. I'm not even legally a mammal.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg explains why he shouldn't be in the cold
jokes.punchline: Escalates from biological facts to absurd legal classification
jokes.explanation: Great escalation structure and perfect Zoidberg character integration - the legal mammal classification is unexpectedly funny

jokes.text: Huh, learn something dumb every day.
jokes.setup: Fry mentions being frozen for 1000 years
jokes.punchline: Zapp's twist on 'learn something new every day' reveals his attitude toward information
jokes.explanation: Nice wordplay subversion that fits Zapp's character perfectly - economical and character-specific

jokes.text: I'm wearing a belly parka.
jokes.setup: Amy asks to get inside because she's cold
jokes.punchline: Reveals she's wearing clothing designed for her exposed midriff fashion
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy concept that plays on Amy's signature midriff-baring outfit, though the setup is straightforward

jokes.text: If I understand ice fishing, and I'm pretty sure I don't, we'll need to laser-blast a hole in the ice.
jokes.setup: Fry offers advice on ice fishing technique
jokes.punchline: Admits complete ignorance while confidently giving wrong futuristic solution
jokes.explanation: Classic Fry character moment - the self-aware admission of ignorance followed by confident wrong advice is perfectly executed

jokes.text: I don't see any irony in that.
jokes.setup: Zapp tells Bender to use his robot strength to drill manually instead of using technology
jokes.punchline: Bender completely misses the obvious irony of a robot doing manual labor
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender character comedy - his literal-mindedness about irony while being the living embodiment of it

jokes.text: Cocoa is for yodeling babies.
jokes.setup: Someone suggests hot cocoa break
jokes.punchline: Zapp's inexplicably specific dismissal combining infants and alpine singing
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly random insult that's both nonsensical and oddly specific - very quotable and memorable

jokes.text: If you're sober, it isn't ice fishing.
jokes.setup: Discussion of proper ice fishing protocol
jokes.punchline: Zapp reveals his understanding that alcohol is essential to the activity
jokes.explanation: Solid observational humor about ice fishing culture, though not particularly original

jokes.text: We'll check it again at halftime.
jokes.setup: Taking Fry's temperature while he's very sick
jokes.punchline: Zapp treats the fever reading like a sports score he's following
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment showing Zapp's complete lack of empathy and sports obsession overtaking medical concern

jokes.text: Nobody cut me any slack when I broke my spine. Didn't even take a day off of work. Lot of people depend on you when you sell trampolines door to door.
jokes.setup: Dad criticizes Fry for taking sick day after falling through ice
jokes.punchline: Reveals his 'important' job was selling trampolines door-to-door despite broken spine
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation structure - builds up his dedication then reveals the absurdly unimportant job, great character development

jokes.text: I'm not sure science is your thing, seeing how that chimp at the zoo tricked you out of your allowance.
jokes.setup: Dad doubts Fry's scientific abilities
jokes.punchline: Cites specific embarrassing incident of being outsmarted by a zoo animal
jokes.explanation: Great specific detail that perfectly establishes both Fry's gullibility and his father's harsh parenting style

jokes.text: Yeah, well, I'll show you and Mr. Bananas.
jokes.setup: Response to being told a chimp tricked him
jokes.punchline: Fry remembers and names the chimp, showing he took it personally
jokes.explanation: Nice quick callback that shows Fry's wounded pride and tendency to hold grudges against animals

jokes.text: You're holding it, genius.
jokes.setup: Fry asks where the contest flyer is
jokes.punchline: Yancy points out Fry is literally holding what he's looking for
jokes.explanation: Standard absent-minded moment for Fry, economical but predictable

jokes.text: Could it be taken from you if you were, say, unconscious?
jokes.setup: Learning about the valuable common cold virus
jokes.punchline: Bender immediately considers robbing an unconscious sick person
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender character moment - immediately sees criminal opportunity in friend's illness with typical directness

jokes.text: Oh, I try to act cool, but inside I'm freaking out.
jokes.setup: Farnsworth claims he's always lived in terror of this happening
jokes.punchline: Admits his calm exterior is completely fake
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment showing Farnsworth's tendency toward dramatic revelation about his hidden emotions

jokes.text: Now, now, let's not all panic at once. We'll have to take shifts.
jokes.setup: Everyone starts panicking about the quarantine
jokes.punchline: Farnsworth suggests organizing the panic in an orderly fashion
jokes.explanation: Excellent deadpan humor - treating panic as a logistical problem to be managed efficiently is perfectly absurd and well-crafted

jokes.text: Huh, so it was hiding deep down inside of me, like my skeleton bones?
jokes.setup: Learning the cold virus survived frozen inside him
jokes.punchline: Fry compares it to his skeleton bones as if that's not obvious anatomy
jokes.explanation: Classic Fry stupidity - treating basic anatomy as a profound comparison point shows his unique thought process

jokes.text: It could kill millions or nobody. I suppose it might even bring a few people back to life. Anything is possible in science.
jokes.setup: Asked how deadly the virus could be
jokes.punchline: Gives completely unhelpful range including resurrection, justified by 'anything is possible'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Farnsworth character moment - his scientific authority used to justify complete non-answers with escalating absurdity

jokes.text: My nose is all gunked up with blork. And I've got a hankering for a useless remedy created by a schoolteacher.
jokes.setup: Leela describes her cold symptoms
jokes.punchline: Uses nonsense word 'blork' and dismisses home remedies while craving them
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay with 'blork' and good observation about craving remedies you know don't work

jokes.text: Yesterday I was nearly killed by a tight hat.
jokes.setup: Farnsworth worries what the cold will do to his fragile health
jokes.punchline: Reveals he was almost killed by something completely benign
jokes.explanation: Excellent character-specific humor highlighting Farnsworth's extreme frailty in an absurd, memorable way

jokes.text: Which is why we'll all be counting on you and you alone to nurse us through this catastrophe. Why, God? Why have I been singled out?
jokes.setup: Being told he's immune and needed to help everyone
jokes.punchline: Bender treats being healthy and needed as a divine punishment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender selfishness - being needed to help others is his worst nightmare, excellent character integration

jokes.text: Face it, someone with your limited brainpower would be lucky to get a job as a pizza delivery boy.
jokes.setup: Dad criticizes Fry's intelligence and future prospects
jokes.punchline: Predicts exactly the job Fry actually had in the future
jokes.explanation: Great dramatic irony for series fans - the insult is exactly what Fry became, adding poignancy to the harsh parenting

jokes.text: Man, I have got to go back to law school.
jokes.setup: Obama delivers pizza in 1988 as a college job
jokes.punchline: References his real-life path from law school to presidency
jokes.explanation: Nice historical reference joke that works on multiple levels for viewers who recognize the young Obama

jokes.text: Pi over four radians of pizza, please. What is that, about a slice? More like exactly a slice.
jokes.setup: Gedgie orders pizza using mathematical terms
jokes.punchline: Corrects the approximation to emphasize mathematical precision
jokes.explanation: Good character establishment for the genius rival - shows his precision and mathematical mindset in everyday situations

jokes.text: When I was shot down over Korea, I had to eat Kimchi. He was our interpreter.
jokes.setup: Zapp mentions eating something bad in Korea
jokes.punchline: Reveals 'Kimchi' was actually a person, not the Korean food
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy with perfect misdirection - audience expects food complaint, gets cannibalism revelation. Superb structure and shock value

jokes.text: Chicken, water, fire. You take it from there.
jokes.setup: Sick crew asks for chicken soup
jokes.punchline: Bender provides ingredients list instead of actually cooking
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender laziness - technically fulfills request while doing absolutely nothing helpful

jokes.text: Without turning my head, I'd say 100%.
jokes.setup: Asked about containment status while Bender escapes behind him
jokes.punchline: Gives confident assessment while literally not looking at what he's guarding
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy setup with nice ironic confidence from incompetent guard

jokes.text: That robot was a heck of a nice feller.
jokes.setup: Bender just infected multiple guards by shaking hands
jokes.punchline: Guard praises Bender's friendliness while being unknowingly infected
jokes.explanation: Nice dramatic irony - audience knows Bender just infected them while guard appreciates his 'kindness'

jokes.text: So what's your project-- the effects of losing on idiots?
jokes.setup: Gedgie asks about Fry's science project
jokes.punchline: Implies Fry will lose and calls him an idiot in same insult
jokes.explanation: Standard smart-kid insult, economical but not particularly creative

jokes.text: You are an evil, evil nerd.
jokes.setup: Gedgie steals Fry's idea and improves it
jokes.punchline: Fry's comeback emphasizes the 'evil' while still acknowledging intelligence
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment showing Fry's limited insult vocabulary while acknowledging the rival's intelligence

jokes.text: All citizens are advised to wash their hands thoroughly after beating up Philip J. Fry.
jokes.setup: News report about containing the virus
jokes.punchline: Public health advice assumes citizens will be assaulting Fry
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor combining public health messaging with assumed mob violence - well-structured absurdist joke

jokes.text: The situation is expected to deteriorate as newscasters whip the city into a panic.
jokes.setup: News report about the crisis
jokes.punchline: News acknowledges its own role in creating panic while doing exactly that
jokes.explanation: Sharp media satire - self-aware acknowledgment of how news creates the problems it reports on

jokes.text: We don't have nearly enough piranhas.
jokes.setup: Discussing protocol 62 as an option
jokes.punchline: Reveals one containment protocol involves piranhas, which they lack
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd government contingency plan - the casual mention of piranha shortage is perfectly ridiculous

jokes.text: I'm just glad I have my wool scarf. What good is a wool...?
jokes.setup: Amy tries to stay positive as they're flown into the sun
jokes.punchline: Realizes mid-sentence that wool won't help against solar incineration
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Amy's optimism crashing into reality mid-thought

jokes.text: Wern... strom!
jokes.setup: Wernstrom is introduced as surgeon general
jokes.punchline: Farnsworth's signature dramatic pronunciation of his rival's name
jokes.explanation: Classic series running gag - Farnsworth's dramatic way of saying his rival's name always gets a response from fans

jokes.text: He consented! You all heard it!
jokes.setup: Fry agreeing to be ground up for vaccine
jokes.punchline: Wernstrom immediately legalizes the murder with witnesses
jokes.explanation: Good dark comedy about consent and murder. Wernstrom's eager legal interpretation of Fry's sacrifice.

jokes.text: He already survived in the vacuum of my mom. I mean, my mom's vacuum.
jokes.setup: Asked if guinea pig can survive in vacuum of space
jokes.punchline: Fry accidentally makes inappropriate comment then awkwardly corrects it
jokes.explanation: Classic accidental innuendo joke with typical Fry awkward recovery

jokes.text: Oh! I don't know why I even build these things.
jokes.setup: Farnsworth's invention fails to work as intended
jokes.punchline: His standard complaint about his own inventions
jokes.explanation: Standard running gag - reliable character moment but not particularly creative

jokes.text: Thin and crispy, way too risky!
jokes.setup: Fry needs to break through ice to reach satellite
jokes.punchline: Uses Zapp's ice safety rhyme from earlier, now with proper pronunciation
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that shows character growth - Fry learned the lesson and now pronounces it correctly

jokes.text: I could have used an introduction like that when I first met the moon men.
jokes.setup: Buzz Aldrin gets flowery introduction
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions meeting moon men as if it's historical fact
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist humor using real astronaut to deliver completely fictional moon encounter as casual aside

jokes.text: But seriously, there are moon men.
jokes.setup: Previous moon men comment seemed like a joke
jokes.punchline: Buzz doubles down, insisting it's serious information
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation - the deadpan serious delivery makes the absurd claim even funnier

jokes.text: Besides, Buzz Aldrin ran over my guinea pig in the parking lot.
jokes.setup: Fry explaining why his experiment wouldn't have worked anyway
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of famous astronaut committing vehicular guinea pig slaughter
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd throwaway line. The casual delivery of Buzz Aldrin's hit-and-run on a science project is perfectly dark.

jokes.text: Besides, Buzz Aldrin ran over my guinea pig in the parking lot.
jokes.setup: Fry reflects on how his experiment wouldn't have worked anyway
jokes.punchline: Reveals the famous astronaut accidentally killed his test subject
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd and dark - turning a space hero into an accidental guinea pig killer is perfectly unexpected

jokes.text: Now I'll give you the shot.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg says getting blood will hurt
jokes.punchline: Implies the painful part was drawing blood, shot will be worse
jokes.explanation: Standard Zoidberg medical incompetence joke - predictable but fits character

jokes.text: I could have flip-flopped out.
jokes.setup: Zapp mentions Fry hasn't thanked him for rescue
jokes.punchline: Fry claims he could have escaped drowning by flip-flopping like a fish
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Fry's stubborn pride and absurd problem-solving logic

jokes.text: Some day, you may face adversities so preposterous, I can't even conceive of them. But I know you'll pull through and make me proud.
jokes.setup: Father gives touching speech about Fry's future challenges
jokes.punchline: Unknowingly predicts Fry's ridiculous future adventures with perfect accuracy
jokes.explanation: Excellent dramatic irony - perfectly describes Fry's future without knowing it, beautiful character development and series meta-commentary
